steps.title: Ignore the appositive
steps.content: The subject is The Proto-Uralic language, which is singular. The descriptive phrase about being the common ancestor of many languages interrupts the sentence but does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Choose singular present tense
steps.content: A, B, and C all use plural verb forms that cannot agree with the singular subject. The sentence also describes a general fact, so the singular present-tense verb is needed. The answer is D.
